hypothesis: If the valency calculation bug (treating aromatic bonds as order 1 instead of 1.5) is corrected and chemically accurate valency lookup tables are used alongside a quantum mechanical geometry evaluation protocol (GFN2-xTB rather than MMFF94), then reported validity and geometry quality metrics for state-of-the-art 3D molecule generation models will change substantially from previously published values, because the original benchmarking methodology systematically misclassified chemically invalid molecules as valid and used a force field incapable of accurately representing the reference geometries in GEOM-Drugs.
